creator: aturetta@bestunion.it
text:
Martin Blapp wrote:
> 
> Hi Angelo,
> 
> I've committed parts of your patch and part of Jimmy Olgenis
> patch. There were some parts which I did not understand. Can
> you generate a new version of your patch and comment what you
> did exactly ? Sorry for the additional work !

The change in post-install is a simplification fix to the installation 
process. The current port makefile installs some files by hand, but the 
open-vm-tools makefile already installs the same files correctly: I 
suppose this is a leftover from a previous version. Moreover, the 
'kldxref' is only run if $OSREL >= 6, which is obviously wrong.
I also added a pkg-message that explains the rc.conf variables, and 
parameterized the rc script file per kernel version (as some modules are 
not installed in FreeBSD-5)

Ah, and I updated the CONFLICTS directive, making open-vm-tools and 
open-vm-tools-nox11 mutually exclusive and removing a portlint(1) 
warning about the current CONFLICTS.

BTW, having seen the use of %%LIB32DIR%% for kernel modules, I added the 
needed substitutions also to Makefile and files/vmware-kmod.sh.in

Please find attached an updated patch, which brings my changes to the 
current port. I added this patch to the open bug, so you can actually 
close it after commit. Proposed commit message:

============================================================
- pet portlint(1)
- add info on the rc.conf settings
- don't reinstall files twice
- open-vm-tools and open-vm-tools-nox11  need to CONFLICT each other
- on 5.x, the scripts won't try to load unsupported kmods
- kldxref should be run also on 5.x
- bump PORTREVISION

Submitted by:	Angelo Turetta <aturetta@bestunion.it>
PR:		127346
============================================================

(remember to actually 'cvs rm pkg-plist-x11', currently a 0 bytes file 
has been committed)


Hope this helps,
Angelo.
